{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Officer",
  "description": "Ekris Bio Health Foods Nigeria Limited is an innovative, indigenous food manufacturing and packaging company proudly rooted in Nigeria. Our mission is to bridge the gap between Nigeria&rsquo;s rich and diverse indigenous soups and the often substandard quality of existing swallow products in the market.

We believe that our swallows should serve as the perfect complement&mdash;indeed, a rallying point&mdash;for our traditional soups, enhancing both taste and nutritional value. Beyond swallows, we are committed to showcasing the versatility of healthy flour-based products to Nigerians and the wider African market. Our product development strategy focuses on unlocking a broad spectrum of applications for our nutritious flour blends across multiple food categories.
Accordingly, we are specifically devoted to the research, development, and production of healthy alternative Swallows, Beverages, Cereals, and Confectionery products designed to promote improved dietary choices and overall well-being.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type:&nbsp;Full-Time
Industry: FMCG (Food Manufacturing &amp; Packaging)

About Us


	We are a foremost food manufacturing and packaging company in the FMCG sector, committed to operational excellence, efficient distribution, and timely delivery of quality products.
	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Logistics Officer to join our operations team in Lagos State.


Job Summary


	The Logistics Officer will be responsible for coordinating and managing the movement of goods, ensuring efficient distribution, and maintaining optimal inventory and delivery processes.
	The ideal candidate must be highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of optimizing logistics operations to support business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily logistics and distribution operations.
	Plan and manage product transportation from warehouse to customers and distributors.
	Monitor inventory levels and ensure accurate stock records.
	Liaise with warehouse, sales, and production teams to ensure smooth order fulfillment.
	Track shipments and ensure timely delivery of goods.
	Manage relationships with transport vendors and service providers.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety standards, and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare logistics reports and analyze data to improve efficiency and reduce costs.
	Handle delivery issues, discrepancies, and customer complaints related to logistics.
	Optimize routes and delivery schedules to enhance operational efficiency.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of relevant experience in logistics or supply chain, preferably within FMCG.
	Strong understanding of distribution, inventory management, and transportation processes.
	Proficiency in logistics and inventory management systems.
	Good working knowledge of Microsoft Excel and reporting tools.
	Strong problem-solving and organizational skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.


Key Competencies:


	Strong organizational and planning skills
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Effective communication and coordination skills
	Analytical and problem-solving ability
	Time management and multitasking
	Ability to work independently and within a team.


What We Offer


	Salary: N250,000 - N300,000 / month.&nbsp;
	Competitive salary (based on experience)
	Career growth and development opportunities
	Structured and professional work environment
	Performance-based incentives.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;michealani@zohomail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Logistics Officer &ndash; Lagos State&quot;&nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

Logistics Officer Job at Ekris Bio Health Foods Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Ekris Bio Health Foods Nigeria Limited is an innovative, indigenous food manufacturing and packaging company proudly rooted in Nigeria. Our mission is to bridge the gap between Nigeria’s rich and diverse indigenous soups and the often substandard quality of existing swallow products in the market.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We believe that our swallows should serve as the perfect complement—indeed, a rallying point—for our traditional soups, enhancing both taste and nutritional value. Beyond swallows, we are committed to showcasing the versatility of healthy flour-based products to Nigerians and the wider African market. Our product development strategy focuses on unlocking a broad spectrum of applications for our nutritious flour blends across multiple food categories.
Accordingly, we are specifically devoted to the research, development, and production of healthy alternative Swallows, Beverages, Cereals, and Confectionery products designed to promote improved dietary choices and overall well-being.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time
Industry: FMCG (Food Manufacturing & Packaging)

About Us


	We are a foremost food manufacturing and packaging company in the FMCG sector, committed to operational excellence, efficient distribution, and timely delivery of quality products.
	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Logistics Officer to join our operations team in Lagos State.


Job Summary


	The Logistics Officer will be responsible for coordinating and managing the movement of goods, ensuring efficient distribution, and maintaining optimal inventory and delivery processes.
	The ideal candidate must be highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of optimizing logistics operations to support business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily logistics and distribution operations.
	Plan and manage product transportation from warehouse to customers and distributors.
	Monitor inventory levels and ensure accurate stock records.
	Liaise with warehouse, sales, and production teams to ensure smooth order fulfillment.
	Track shipments and ensure timely delivery of goods.
	Manage relationships with transport vendors and service providers.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety standards, and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare logistics reports and analyze data to improve efficiency and reduce costs.
	Handle delivery issues, discrepancies, and customer complaints related to logistics.
	Optimize routes and delivery schedules to enhance operational efficiency.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or related field.
	3–5 years of relevant experience in logistics or supply chain, preferably within FMCG.
	Strong understanding of distribution, inventory management, and transportation processes.
	Proficiency in logistics and inventory management systems.
	Good working knowledge of Microsoft Excel and reporting tools.
	Strong problem-solving and organizational skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.


Key Competencies:


	Strong organizational and planning skills
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Effective communication and coordination skills
	Analytical and problem-solving ability
	Time management and multitasking
	Ability to work independently and within a team.


What We Offer


	Salary: N250,000 - N300,000 / month. 
	Competitive salary (based on experience)
	Career growth and development opportunities
	Structured and professional work environment
	Performance-based incentives.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Logistics Officer – Lagos State" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Public Relations Officer",
  "description": "Vitel Wireless Limited is a fast-growing telecommunications company and a licensed Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) in Nigeria. We provide reliable voice, data, and SMS services nationwide, leveraging strong partnerships with major network operators. Driven by innovation and a commitment to quality service, we are focused on expanding connectivity and delivering affordable telecom solutions across Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Public Relations Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview


	As we continue to expand our presence in Lagos, we are seeking a proactive and creative Public Relations professional to help strengthen our brand visibility and manage our corporate communications.
	The Public Relations Officer will be responsible for managing the company&rsquo;s public image, building strong relationships with media and stakeholders, and supporting communication strategies that enhance brand awareness. This role involves developing PR campaigns, coordinating media engagements, and ensuring consistent messaging across all communication channels.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement public relations strategies to promote the company&rsquo;s brand and services.
	Build and maintain relationships with media outlets, journalists, and key stakeholders.
	Draft press releases, media statements, newsletters, and other corporate communications.
	Monitor media coverage and prepare reports on public perception and brand reputation.
	Coordinate media interviews, press briefings, and corporate events.
	Support internal communications and ensure consistent brand messaging.
	Manage crisis communication and respond to public or media inquiries when necessary.
	Collaborate with marketing and management teams to align communication initiatives with business objectives.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Public Relations, Communications, Journalism, Marketing, or a related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of experience in public relations, corporate communications, or media relations.
	Strong writing, editing, and storytelling skills.
	Excellent interpersonal and relationship-building abilities.
	Experience working with media platforms and digital communication channels.
	Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary;&nbsp;N80,000 per month.&nbsp;
	Innovative and collaborative work environment.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	The chance to contribute to Nigeria&rsquo;s first MVNO and the future of digital connectivity.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to:&nbsp;sandra.aballa@vitelwireless.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;&ldquo;Application &ndash; Public Relations&rdquo;&nbsp;as the subject of the mail.",

Public Relations Officer Job at Vitel Wireless Limited




        
    

        
        Vitel Wireless Limited is a fast-growing telecommunications company and a licensed Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) in Nigeria. We provide reliable voice, data, and SMS services nationwide, leveraging strong partnerships with major network operators. Driven by innovation and a commitment to quality service, we are focused on expanding connectivity and delivering affordable telecom solutions across Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Public Relations Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	As we continue to expand our presence in Lagos, we are seeking a proactive and creative Public Relations professional to help strengthen our brand visibility and manage our corporate communications.
	The Public Relations Officer will be responsible for managing the company’s public image, building strong relationships with media and stakeholders, and supporting communication strategies that enhance brand awareness. This role involves developing PR campaigns, coordinating media engagements, and ensuring consistent messaging across all communication channels.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement public relations strategies to promote the company’s brand and services.
	Build and maintain relationships with media outlets, journalists, and key stakeholders.
	Draft press releases, media statements, newsletters, and other corporate communications.
	Monitor media coverage and prepare reports on public perception and brand reputation.
	Coordinate media interviews, press briefings, and corporate events.
	Support internal communications and ensure consistent brand messaging.
	Manage crisis communication and respond to public or media inquiries when necessary.
	Collaborate with marketing and management teams to align communication initiatives with business objectives.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Public Relations, Communications, Journalism, Marketing, or a related field.
	3–5 years of experience in public relations, corporate communications, or media relations.
	Strong writing, editing, and storytelling skills.
	Excellent interpersonal and relationship-building abilities.
	Experience working with media platforms and digital communication channels.
	Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary; N80,000 per month. 
	Innovative and collaborative work environment.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	The chance to contribute to Nigeria’s first MVNO and the future of digital connectivity.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using “Application – Public Relations” as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accounts Officer",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Finance Manager / Chief Accountant

Job Summary


	The Accounts Officer is responsible for managing accounts payable processes, inventory accounting, supplier reconciliations, and expense postings.
	The role ensures accuracy in financial records, proper documentation of transactions, and timely reconciliation of supplier accounts in line with company policies and accounting standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Process and account for Local Purchase Orders (LPOs), including Goods Received Notes (GRNs), and perform accurate 3-way matching (PO&ndash;GRN&ndash;Invoice).
	Record inventory receipts and ensure proper documentation and valuation.
	Perform detailed supplier account reconciliations by:
	Matching supplier statements with ledger balances
	Identifying and resolving variances such as unposted invoices, debit/credit notes, and timing differences
	Post operating expenses accurately into the accounting system.
	Maintain accuracy and completeness of General Ledger postings related to payables, inventory, and expenses.
	Support month-end and year-end closing activities, including preparation of reconciliations and audit schedules.
	Provide necessary documentation and support for internal and external audits.
	Ensure proper filing and organization of financial records and supporting documents.
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Finance Manager or Chief Accountant.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in accounting, finance, or a related field.
	3-4 years of relevant experience in accounts payable, inventory accounting, or financial operations.
	Experience with ERP / accounting systems is an added advantage.


Competencies (Skills, Abilities &amp; Knowledge):


	Strong knowledge of accounts payable processes and controls.
	Inventory accounting and reconciliation skills.
	Supplier reconciliation and discrepancy resolution.
	General Ledger management.
	Financial reporting and documentation.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Good communication and organizational skills.


Salary
N350,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to:&nbsp;cv@ascentech.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Accounts Officer Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Officer

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Finance Manager / Chief Accountant

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Accounts Officer is responsible for managing accounts payable processes, inventory accounting, supplier reconciliations, and expense postings.
	The role ensures accuracy in financial records, proper documentation of transactions, and timely reconciliation of supplier accounts in line with company policies and accounting standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Process and account for Local Purchase Orders (LPOs), including Goods Received Notes (GRNs), and perform accurate 3-way matching (PO–GRN–Invoice).
	Record inventory receipts and ensure proper documentation and valuation.
	Perform detailed supplier account reconciliations by:
	Matching supplier statements with ledger balances
	Identifying and resolving variances such as unposted invoices, debit/credit notes, and timing differences
	Post operating expenses accurately into the accounting system.
	Maintain accuracy and completeness of General Ledger postings related to payables, inventory, and expenses.
	Support month-end and year-end closing activities, including preparation of reconciliations and audit schedules.
	Provide necessary documentation and support for internal and external audits.
	Ensure proper filing and organization of financial records and supporting documents.
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Finance Manager or Chief Accountant.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in accounting, finance, or a related field.
	3-4 years of relevant experience in accounts payable, inventory accounting, or financial operations.
	Experience with ERP / accounting systems is an added advantage.


Competencies (Skills, Abilities & Knowledge):


	Strong knowledge of accounts payable processes and controls.
	Inventory accounting and reconciliation skills.
	Supplier reconciliation and discrepancy resolution.
	General Ledger management.
	Financial reporting and documentation.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Good communication and organizational skills.


Salary
N350,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Mandilas Group Limited
content:
Mandilas Group Limited is a conglomerate of companies with branches in different parts of the country. Our Core activities include Engineering / Air conditioning Sales, Installation and Service, Plumbing and Automobile Sales / Leasing / Service.

We seek to recruit individuals with strong professional and academic credentials with cognate years of experience who will deliver value to stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Used Car Sales Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This role is primarily responsible for the general management of the pre-owned vehicles business, including sourcing (local purchase, import and consignment), storage, marketing and sales for reasonable profitability.
	Essentially, the role will research, develop and implement pre-owned auto sales plans.


Job Scope and Responsibilities
Business Development, Management and Reporting:


	Support the General Manager, Mobility to develop and achieve used vehicles sales business objectives
	Develop and implement annual strategic business plans for used vehicles sales operations
	Identify business opportunities by researching and analyzing existing and potential customer needs
	Liaise with GM Mobility to set sales targets and KPIs for pre-owned sales team to effectively manage sales activities
	Develop a marketing plan for attracting new customers and retaining the existing ones.
	Ensure the development and implementation of sales strategies
	Develop proposals and quotations for existing and prospective customers
	Conduct sales presentations to customers on Mandilas’ new car products
	Possess a rich bouquet of local and international sources for pre-owned automobiles
	Analyze the market potential and determine the value of existing and prospective customers to the business
	Analyze trends, data, demographics, pricing strategies, and other information that can potentially improve sales performance
	Develop sales team industry skills and knowledge to increase market share
	Participate in trade shows and conventions to showcase products and services
	Provide regular performance reports to GM Mobility on customer needs, problems, interests, competitive activities, and potential for new and existing products and services


Stakeholder and External Relations:


	Build and maintain excellent relationships with key account holders by making periodic visits; exploring specific needs and anticipating new opportunities
	Research and develop marketing programs by consulting with internal departments and external suppliers
	Organize interactive sessions for existing, prospective and potential clients to drive interaction and provide more confidence in Mandilas pre-owned mobility products and services


Customer Service:


	Design and maintain effective customer complaints handling procedures
	Design, implement and continually review the customer satisfaction system in relation to pre-owned vehicle sales
	Design appropriate customer loyalty programs, review and analyze results and make recommendations to GM Mobility as applicable
	Advise customers on available vehicle options, care and maintenance


Leadership and People Management:


	Provide strong leadership and ensure clear strategic objectives are in place.
	Develop job knowledge by participating in educational opportunities; reading professional publications; maintaining personal networks and participating in professional organizations.
	Support the implementation and facilitation of relevant workshops and training courses
	Ensure continuous design and deployment of training curriculum for pre-owned auto sales personnel
	Effectively manage personnel within the department to reduce costs while attaining the strategic goals and objectives of the business
	Promote Mandilas core values (Integrity, Care for the future, Accountability, Reliability, Respect for people and Excellence) and a strong team culture
	Manage the day-to-day performance, learning and development of the team, including one-on-one feedback sessions and conduct effective performance appraisals
	Coach and mentor team members towards delivering outstanding pre-owned auto sales experience to customers.


Education and Experience
Education:


	Minimum of first Degree in Marketing, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics or any business-related discipline.
	An MBA or other commercial or management postgraduate degrees will be an advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of six (6) years mobility sales experience with proven track record of not less than three (3) years in the pre-owned space in Nigeria.


Salary
N500,000 - N750,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Procurement Manager (Technical)

Job Location: Lagos

Scope and Responsibilities


	Develop and implement procurement strategies for technical materials, including HVAC equipment, spare parts, compressors, refrigerants, and automotive components
	Source and evaluate suppliers for HVAC systems, tools, and automobile parts, ensuring quality, reliability, and cost competitiveness
	Manage procurement lifecycle: requisition, RFQ/RFP, evaluation, negotiation, purchase order issuance, and delivery follow-up
	Lead technical vendor negotiations, including pricing, warranties, service-level agreements (SLAs), and delivery timelines
	Collaborate with engineering and service teams to define technical specifications and requirements
	Ensure compliance with company procurement policies, industry standards, and regulatory requirements
	Develop and maintain a qualified vendor database for HVAC and automotive categories
	Monitor supplier performance and implement vendor evaluation and improvement plans
	Optimize inventory levels in coordination with stores to prevent stock-outs or overstocking of critical parts
	Support project teams with timely procurement of materials for installations and maintenance
	Track market trends, pricing benchmarks, and technological advancements in HVAC and automotive sectors
	Manage import processes where required (documentation, duties, logistics coordination)
	Maintain accurate procurement records and prepare periodic reports on cost savings and supplier performance
	Ensure adherence to HSE standards in procurement of technical materials
	Perform any other duties as assigned by management.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Administrative Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Scope and Responsibilities
Office Administration:


	Manage day-to-day office operations to ensure smooth workflow and efficiency.
	Monitor and maintain adequate levels of office supplies and consumables.
	Coordinate office logistics including meeting arrangements, workspace allocation, and general office upkeep.
	Maintain proper filing systems (physical and electronic) for records and documentation.
	Support internal communication and dissemination of administrative notices.


Facility Management:


	Assist with supervising maintenance and functionality of office facilities, including HVAC systems, electricals, plumbing, and general infrastructure.
	Coordinate preventive and corrective maintenance activities.
	Ensure workplace health, safety, and environmental standards are maintained.
	Liaise with facility service providers (cleaning, security, waste management, etc.) to ensure service delivery standards are met.
	Monitor utility usage (power, water, diesel, etc.) and optimize cost efficiency.


Vendor & Contract Management:


	Assist in sourcing, onboarding, and managing vendors/service providers.
	Monitor service level agreements (SLAs) and ensure compliance.
	Process vendor invoices and maintain accurate records of payments and contracts.


Compliance & Reporting:


	Ensure compliance with company policies and relevant regulatory requirements.
	Maintain records related to facility operations, maintenance schedules, and inspections.
	Provide periodic reports on administrative and facility activities.


General Responsibilities:


	Support the Administrative Manager in implementing policies and procedures.
	Assist in managing office support staff where applicable.
	Participate in administrative projects and continuous improvement initiatives.
	Perform any other duties as assigned by management.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Network Security Officer",
  "description": "Unified Payment is a fast-leading financial technology company that provides seamless, secure, and efficient digital payment solutions. We enable businesses and individuals to send, receive, and manage payments across multiple channels through a single, integrated platform, driving financial inclusion, operational efficiency, and innovation in the digital payments ecosystem.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Security Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Objectives


	Ensure the integrity of the network and security infrastructure of the company.


Duties &amp; Responsibilities


	Ensure Network Connectivity to POS.
	Ensure network connectivity to Banks.
	Cleaning and tuning of desktops, laptops and printers of users.
	Ensure all Network devices and systems are PCIDSS compliant as required.
	Ensure Internet access connectivity for transaction and email processing is efficient.
	Ensure all anti-virus databases are updated frequently as required.
	Ensure that WAN link to DR site is optimal.
	Ensure that all security and communications devices are optimal, functioning with the latest patches and firmware.
	Ensure business continuity and strategic planning on DR site availability.
	Execute information security strategy to proactively identify risk and drive remediation.
	Ensure that all physical and logical controls that govern the data Centre are adhered to.
	Ensure efficient and timely deployment of fixes/patches.
	Ensure that all critical security patches (OS and Database) on all servers are up-to date and tested before applying to the production servers.
	Ensure that all warning and critical event logs are reviewed using the SolarWinds application and escalated to the responsible unit.Ensure that all servers have antivirus software, and the signatures are up to date.
	Ensure that enterprise storage and server firmware is regularly updated to ensure availability and integrity of data.
	Maintain an up-to-date understanding of emerging cyber threats facing epayment institutions.
	Act as a point of contact for managing and delivering various vulnerability and remediation reports.
	Build effective relationships with key stakeholders who support IT Infrastructure, applications, processes and operations.


Requirements
Education:


	Candidates should possess a B.Sc or B.Eng. (Minimum of 2:2) in Electrical / Electronics, Computer Engineering, Computer Science or Physics Electronics.
	An Industry recognized qualification- CCNA &amp; CCNP qualification, MCP, CISM, CompTIA Security, ITIL Foundation V3.


Experience:


	Minimum of 7 years of experience in network and security administration.


Skills / Competencies:


	Fluency in Network Administration
	Good Knowledge of Routing and Switching &bull; Proficiency in administration and network design skills
	Knowledge of Virtualization technology (Citrix, VMware or Hyper-V) &bull; Good Knowledge of monitoring software especially SolarWinds
	General Knowledge of Server Hardware
	Knowledge of operating systems (Linux &amp; Windows).


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;mercy.joseph@up-ng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Network Security Officer Job at Unified Payment




        
    

        
        Unified Payment is a fast-leading financial technology company that provides seamless, secure, and efficient digital payment solutions. We enable businesses and individuals to send, receive, and manage payments across multiple channels through a single, integrated platform, driving financial inclusion, operational efficiency, and innovation in the digital payments ecosystem.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Security Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Objectives
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Ensure the integrity of the network and security infrastructure of the company.


Duties & Responsibilities


	Ensure Network Connectivity to POS.
	Ensure network connectivity to Banks.
	Cleaning and tuning of desktops, laptops and printers of users.
	Ensure all Network devices and systems are PCIDSS compliant as required.
	Ensure Internet access connectivity for transaction and email processing is efficient.
	Ensure all anti-virus databases are updated frequently as required.
	Ensure that WAN link to DR site is optimal.
	Ensure that all security and communications devices are optimal, functioning with the latest patches and firmware.
	Ensure business continuity and strategic planning on DR site availability.
	Execute information security strategy to proactively identify risk and drive remediation.
	Ensure that all physical and logical controls that govern the data Centre are adhered to.
	Ensure efficient and timely deployment of fixes/patches.
	Ensure that all critical security patches (OS and Database) on all servers are up-to date and tested before applying to the production servers.
	Ensure that all warning and critical event logs are reviewed using the SolarWinds application and escalated to the responsible unit.Ensure that all servers have antivirus software, and the signatures are up to date.
	Ensure that enterprise storage and server firmware is regularly updated to ensure availability and integrity of data.
	Maintain an up-to-date understanding of emerging cyber threats facing epayment institutions.
	Act as a point of contact for managing and delivering various vulnerability and remediation reports.
	Build effective relationships with key stakeholders who support IT Infrastructure, applications, processes and operations.


Requirements
Education:


	Candidates should possess a B.Sc or B.Eng. (Minimum of 2:2) in Electrical / Electronics, Computer Engineering, Computer Science or Physics Electronics.
	An Industry recognized qualification- CCNA & CCNP qualification, MCP, CISM, CompTIA Security, ITIL Foundation V3.


Experience:


	Minimum of 7 years of experience in network and security administration.


Skills / Competencies:


	Fluency in Network Administration
	Good Knowledge of Routing and Switching • Proficiency in administration and network design skills
	Knowledge of Virtualization technology (Citrix, VMware or Hyper-V) • Good Knowledge of monitoring software especially SolarWinds
	General Knowledge of Server Hardware
	Knowledge of operating systems (Linux & Windows).


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
